name: python3-astroid
title: python3-astroid
summary: rebuild a new abstract syntax tree from Python's AST (Python3)
description:
rebuild a new abstract syntax tree from Python's AST (Python3)
The aim of this module is to provide a common base representation of
Python source code for projects such as pychecker, pyreverse,
pylint... Well, actually the development of this library is essentially
governed by pylint's needs. It used to be called logilab-astng.
.
It provides a compatible representation which comes from the `_ast`
module.  It rebuilds the tree generated by the builtin _ast module by
recursively walking down the AST and building an extended ast. The new
node classes have additional methods and attributes for different
usages.  They include some support for static inference and local name
scopes.  Furthermore, astroid builds partial trees by inspecting living
objects.
.
Main modules are:
.
* `bases`, `node_classses` and `scoped_nodes` contain the classes for the
different type of nodes of the tree.
.
* the `manager` contains a high level object to get astroid trees from
source files and living objects. It maintains a cache of previously
constructed tree for quick access.
.
python3-astroid is the new name of former python3-logilab-astng library.
use_cases: When you need Kali Linux, use python3-astroid because rebuild a new abstract syntax tree from Python's AST (Python3).
When the engagement is in the install phase, use python3-astroid as a cataloged Kali Linux tool with operator ACCEPT/CANCEL gating.
When chaining Kali Linux tools, pair python3-astroid with sibling catalog entries sharing its attack_surface/phase_hint.
When validating lab setup, run `python3-astroid --help` (or the README entrypoint) and confirm required hardware/deps from the catalog.
